item.title: Russia Plans to Launch Crewed Spacecraft From Baikonur in July
item.description: Russia plans its first launch of a manned space mission from the Baikonur launch site this year with a crew that includes a NASA astronaut.

item.title: Deutsche Bank Chairman Pay Hike Rejected as Too Much by Investor
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG’s plan to give a big pay raise to Supervisory Board Chairman Alexander Wynaendts drew criticism at the lender’s annual general meeting.

item.title: What It Takes to Get a Job at Anthropic
item.description: To win a coveted role, candidates shouldn’t outsource their thinking to AI — and should be prepared to talk about their worldview.

item.title: Venezuelans Back Dollarization to Rein in 600% Inflation Rate
item.description: More than half of Venezuelans support dollarizing the economy to rein in one of the world’s fastest inflation rates.

item.title: Mistral Signs Airbus and BMW as it Brings AI to Manufacturing
item.description: French artificial intelligence startup Mistral AI is expanding into advanced manufacturing, striking deals with new customers Airbus SE and BMW AG as it looks to so-called physical AI to fuel growth.

item.title: What Is Joint Debt and Why Is Europe Talking About It Again?
item.description: Economic pressure from the US and China is forcing some European nations to confront their historic aversion to joint borrowing.

item.title: AtlasEdge Gets €1.2 Billion in Loans for European Data Centers
item.description: A data center operator backed by Liberty Global and DigitalBridge Group Inc. secured more than €1.2 billion ($1.4 billion) in financing to roll out artificial intelligence infrastructure in under-served parts of Europe.

item.title: Zambia Defies Iran War Shock With Inflation Still Cooling
item.description: Zambia’s annual inflation slowed to a more than eight-year low in May as a stronger kwacha and a bumper corn harvest tamed prices.

item.title: Russia Starts Offering Yuan Bonds After Putin’s Visit to Beijing
item.description: Russia started accepting bids for its second sale of yuan-denominated sovereign bonds after President Vladimir Putin held talks in Beijing last week.

item.title: French Power Demand Rises as Deadly Heat Wave Intensifies
item.description: The deadly heat wave gripping northwest Europe is set to intensify in Spain and France, lifting power demand for cooling and threatening fresh temperature records.

item.title: China AI Upstart MiniMax Doubles Sales Ahead of New Model Launch
item.description: MiniMax Group Inc.’s annualized revenue more than doubled over the past two months, as the Chinese AI upstart plans to roll out its next flagship model to entice developers and enterprise clients.

item.title: For International Fintechs, America Is the Grand Prize
item.description: Revolut, Nubank and Wise are among the overseas firms competing for consumers’ dollars in the world’s biggest market.

item.title: Brendan Greeley on the Real 500-Year History of the Dollar
item.description: The dollar is older than the US itself.

item.title: Odd Lots: The 500-Year History of the Dollar (Podcast)
item.description: We love talking about money. And of course, we love talking about the dollar, in all its varieties — from bank deposits to eurodollars to stablecoins. But what fundamentally is a dollar and who actually controls it? To understand these questions, you really need to understand how the dollar was born. Journalist (and current Ph.D. candidate in financial history) Brendan Greeley argues not only that the dollar is older than you might suspect, but that the dollar long precedes the United States its

item.title: Nvidia Server Maker WiWynn Sees AI Bottlenecks Beyond Memory
item.description: Wiwynn Corp., one of the biggest makers of Nvidia Corp. servers, warned of shortages developing in vital data center components beyond just memory chips, potentially slowing or inflating the cost of a global AI infrastructure buildout.

item.title: SocGen CEO Krupa Says Bank’s Efficiency Is ‘at Bottom of Class’
item.description: Societe Generale SA Chief Executive Officer Slawomir Krupa, who’s been at the top for three years, vowed to keep restructuring the bank as it continues to lag in some areas.

item.title: Fish-Feed Maker BioMar Rises in Biggest Denmark IPO in Years
item.description: BioMar Group A/S rose in its first day of trading after Copenhagen’s largest initial public offering since Netcompany Group A/S listed in 2018.

item.title: Four in 10 Wealthy Asian Boomers Lack Succession Plans, Survey Shows
item.description: Asia’s rich baby boomers are failing to discuss wealth transfer with family members or establish formal governance structures despite wanting to preserve fortunes across generations, according to a survey from a Geneva-based private bank.

item.title: KKR Sets Up Milan Office to Target Growth in Dealmaking Activity
item.description: KKR & Co. is opening an office in Milan, part of the firm’s plan to tap the growing wealth market and investment opportunities in Italy’s financial capital.

item.title: HDFC Capital Advisors Aims to Double Fund Base to $9 Billion
item.description: HDFC Capital Advisors Ltd. is looking to double its current fund base of about $4.5 billion over the next three years, its Chief Executive Officer said, as the closely held firm seeks to build on the recent investment from the Development Bank of Japan Inc.

item.title: Nomura Raises Annual Profit Target 50% After Record Year
item.description: Nomura Holdings Inc. raised its profit targets following a year of record earnings, as Chief Executive Officer Kentaro Okuda pushes for stable growth.

item.title: Lagarde Says Central-Bank Credibility Is Key in New World Order
item.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said that governments might be inclined to tighten their grip on monetary authorities and stressed that upholding central-bank independence is even more important in an increasingly challenging global order.

item.title: Stonepeak, Lim Family Said to Weigh Withdrawing Yinson Bid
item.description: Yinson Holdings Bhd.’s founding Lim family and Stonepeak Partners are considering withdrawing a plan to take the energy infrastructure company private, according to people familiar with the information.

item.title: JD.com Founder Vows to Protect Chinese Jobs From AI and Robots
item.description: JD.com Inc. founder Liu Qiangdong vowed to prevent the e-commerce firm’s 900,000-strong workforce from losing their jobs to automation, seeking to allay growing fears that the adoption of AI and robotics could replace workers.

item.title: UK’s Biggest Utility Set to Miss Its 2030 Green Power Target
item.description: SSE Plc said it is unlikely to meet its 2030 renewable energy target, citing a difficult market environment, policy uncertainty and delays to grid connections.

item.title: Taiwan Stock Exchange Plans Longer Trading Hours, Odd-Lot Reform
item.description: The Taiwan Stock Exchange is working on improving a key trading system popular among retail investors and planning longer trading hours to attract more demand, the bourse’s chairman said.

item.title: FTSE 100 and Gilts Fall After Iran Strikes
Iceland Ousts Switzerland as World’s Priciest Nation, Union Says
item.description: Iceland is again the world’s most expensive country, surpassing Switzerland for the first time in years, calculations by a local trade union showed.

item.title: China’s Crop Belt Faces Flood Risk as Heavy Rains Arrive Early
item.description: Heavy rains are arriving earlier and lasting longer across several parts of China this year, raising risks for agriculture and disaster management.

item.title: Africa Startups Turn Inward as US AI Boom Drains Venture Capital
item.description: African startups are rewriting their funding playbook as the global artificial intelligence boom pulls venture capital toward the US, forcing emerging markets to compete for cash.
